strategies.title: Value Underflow
strategies.description: Defensive strategy anchored in first-order structural drivers — demographics, resources, credit cycles, monetary aggregates — rather than accounting multiples, applied to overlooked corners of the market. The edge combines real-world dynamics with financial-system metrics for resilience across regimes. No fixed horizon: positions are held as long as the structural thesis holds, exited only on its deterioration.

strategies.title: Trend Spotting
strategies.description: Trend-following strategy that enters once a move is confirmed by price action, flows, and a clear catalyst, but still has meaningful runway. Exits are triggered by anticipated thesis reversal — not price confirmation — to preserve the asymmetry. Capital rotates continuously into the next high-conviction setup, with holdings running 1 to 6 months. Goal: own the middle of the move, where conviction is highest and risk/reward most favourable.

strategies.title: Next Horizon
strategies.description: The strategy takes early, concentrated positions in underappreciated frontier sectors and companies, betting on interpretive depth rather than speed. Exits are triggered by consensus — not thesis breakdown — once the contrarian view becomes mainstream and the early-mover premium is captured. Best-in-class structural winners are an exception: held for years beyond consensus to compound. Horizon is 8 months to several years for the thematic core, longer for retained leaders.
